summary: Classes resume after the Midterm Incident, with Kim Hajin excelling academically and deterring bullies. Nayun, fueled by a competitive spirit, challenges Kim Hajin to a high-stakes bow duel, betting on giving up the bow if she loses. Meanwhile, the Travel Club announces a trip to Paris, where a notorious con artist named Bethell (Berrbet) plans to exploit the cadets as personal guards amidst an assassination threat. Concurrently, Kim Hajin is recruited by the clandestine 'Chameleon Troupe,' a powerful criminal organization, and assigned the significant 'Black' color, indicating his perceived potential within their ranks.
key_moments: Nayun challenges Kim Hajin to a bow duel, with the loser having to give up the bow.
The Travel Club announces a trip to Paris, where a con artist plans to use the cadets as unwitting bodyguards against an assassination threat.
Kim Hajin is recruited into the Chameleon Troupe, a criminal organization, and assigned the 'Black' color, a position of significant power and symbolism within the group.
